items.briefing:
Ransomware threats are escalating, with AI now being used to speed up attacks and shorten the time organisations have to respond. A criminal group called "The Gentlemen" topped ransomware activity rankings in the second quarter of this year, and the Inc ransomware group is actively exploiting security flaws in SonicWall remote access devices — if your organisation uses these, ensure they are patched immediately. Separately, a new report found that 79% of organisations were affected by incidents involving known, unpatched vulnerabilities.

These threats are directly relevant to Australian training organisations, particularly those using remote access tools or running older systems. **Practical tip:** Check with your IT support that all internet-facing devices and software are up to date — most successful attacks exploit vulnerabilities that already have fixes available.

featured.title: DigiCert survey finds 81% of Australian organisations hit by AI security incidents or vulnerabilities
featured.quote: 81% of Australian organisations already experiencing AI-related security incidents signals urgent need for defensive strategies.
